explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: An interview with FM Global CEO Malcolm Roberts indicates a strategic focus on AI from leadership, suggesting established governance for the development and deployment of AI-driven analytics tools. An organizational chart profile further indicates an internal awareness and promotion of AI ethics within the company's analytics leadership, noting past roles in championing data-driven decision-making and promoting ethical AI principles.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This interview with FM Global CEO Malcolm Roberts provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The CEO's discussion of the company's investment in and exploration of an AI-driven analytics tool for climate risk assessment indicates a strategic focus on AI from leadership, suggesting established governance for its development and deployment. Furthermore, the mention of the tool's capabilities and its use for risk quantification implies transparency regarding its function and application.
relevant_sources.title: Interview with FM Global CEO Malcolm Roberts

relevant_sources.summary: This organizational chart profile for Aysun Ceyhan, VP, Head of Analytics & AI Solutions, provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The document indicates an internal awareness and promotion of AI ethics within the company's analytics leadership by noting her past roles in championing data-driven decision-making and promoting ethical AI principles.
relevant_sources.title: Profile of Aysun Ceyhan, VP, Head of Analytics & AI Solutions

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: FM Global's Annual Report 2022 describes how an AI-powered tool creates customized scores for clients to identify and prioritize climate risk improvement efforts, offering insight into the system's capabilities. An interview with the CEO further mentions the capabilities and use of an AI-driven analytics tool for climate risk assessment and risk quantification.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: The FM Global Annual Report 2022 indicates the use of AI, machine learning, and predictive analytics in its Climate Risk Report. This report supports the **transparency** pillar by describing how the AI-powered tool creates a customized score for clients to identify and prioritize climate risk improvement efforts, thus offering insight into the system's capabilities.
relevant_sources.title: FM Global Annual Report 2022

relevant_sources.summary: This interview with FM Global CEO Malcolm Roberts provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The CEO's discussion of the company's investment in and exploration of an AI-driven analytics tool for climate risk assessment indicates a strategic focus on AI from leadership, suggesting established governance for its development and deployment. Furthermore, the mention of the tool's capabilities and its use for risk quantification implies transparency regarding its function and application.
relevant_sources.title: Interview with FM Global CEO Malcolm Roberts

summary.overall_findings: Based on 11 publicly available sources, FM Global's published materials address 2 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars. Transparency and governance are the areas with documented evidence, with the FM Global Annual Report 2022 describing how an AI-powered tool creates a customized score for clients to identify and prioritize climate risk improvement efforts. Additionally, an interview with FM Global CEO Malcolm Roberts indicates a strategic focus on AI from leadership regarding investment in and exploration of an AI-driven analytics tool for climate risk assessment. No qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
